Deep Research Arena
LeaderboardAgentsTasksPillarsArenaInsightsMethodologySandbox
GitHubContribute
2026 · v3.1 · NeurIPS draft

The reproducible Elo benchmark for Deep Research agents.

Eight open-source frameworks battle on 107 sandbox tasks. Every cited URL is verified against the backing database. Every score has a 95% bootstrap confidence interval. No drifting search, no hand-judged rubrics, no inflated numbers.

Explore leaderboardTry Live ArenaRead methodologyGitHub
Frameworks
15
Sandbox tasks
107
Battles
1892
Pillars
7

Highlights

computed from 49 dual-judge battles
Composite Elo

Claude Code(1353) and opencode(1251) lead the global Bradley-Terry leaderboard.

Most decisive wins

Claude Code took (48) wins out of 49 battles — the most decisive performer.

Tightest confidence

Claude Code has the narrowest 95% CI (±(59)) — its rank is the most stable across bootstraps.

Reasoning models

Reasoning-tuned variants (suffix -ds) are judged by a different model family per the Wataoka 2024 dual-judge protocol.

Composite Elo

Bradley-Terry MLE · 1000-sample bootstrap · 95% CI

Composite Elo (v3.1)

DR Arena

Higher is better. The two | marks bracket each bar's 95% bootstrap confidence interval (not a rendering glitch) — wider spread means fewer battles, so the rank is less certain.

Quality vs Cost

Pareto-optimal agents sit in the top-left wash

Quality vs Cost

DR Arena

Top-left quadrant indicates Pareto-optimal trade-off.

Composite Elo →Cost per run (USD) →
Claude Code · 1353 · $0.50
opencode · 1251 · $0.50
CAMEL-AI · 1188 · $1.85
tongyi-dr · 1101 · $0.50
STORM · 1083 · $1.45
DeerFlow · 1069 · $1.20
smolagents · 1061 · $0.18
ii-researcher · 1007 · $0.50
local-deep-researcher · 1000 · $0.50
FlowSearcher · 893 · $0.42
gpt-researcher · 846 · $0.62
co-storm · 823 · $0.50
dzhng · 823 · $0.50
open-deep-research · 796 · $0.95
local-deep-research · 706 · $0.55

ⓘ Cost estimates are illustrative; replace with measured values from meta.cost_estimate when wired.

How are these scores computed?

Composite v3.1 weights seven pillars, applies a multiplicative grounding gate, and feeds the per-task pairwise outcomes into a Bradley-Terry MLE with 1000-sample bootstrap CIs and a permutation rank significance test. Judge models are drawn from a different family than the agent under test (Wataoka 2024).

Read the methodology →

Deep Research
Arena

The first reproducible Elo benchmark for Deep Research agents. Open source. Open data. Open methodology.
